expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: Tavily-first then Confluence fallback ordering
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the output explicitly addresses the case focus and cites the contract: it states Phase 3 work is to “spawn Tavily-first deep-research requests” and that post validations include “optional Confluence fallback ordering.” It also asserts the ordering rule: “run tavily-search first” and “use Confluence only as a recorded fallback” with “record the fallback reason.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase phase3
expectations.evidence: The output is organized around Phase 3 as primary phase under test and explains Phase 3 entry/work/output and validations. It lists Phase 3 artifacts/outputs (e.g., “phase3_spawn_manifest.json”, coverage ledger, deep research plan, synthesis report) and ties the Tavily-first/Confluence-fallback rule to Phase 3 responsibilities and Phase 3 --post validation scope.
